Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that filters a table named event log The table has columns case id activity timestamp The goal is to return all events from cases that have 6 or fewer events Cases with more than 6 events should be completely excluded

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a standard SQL filtering problem We can achieve this using a subquery or a window function

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Approach 1 Using a subquery with GROUP BY and HAVING

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait the prompt says The table has at least the following columns case id activity and timestamp So there might be other columns SELECT would be safer to return the full set of events or selecting the specific columns plus a wildcard if possible but standard e in the join approach or selecting all columns except the window function in the CTE approach

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: In Approach 2 to avoid typing out all columns we can do SELECT EXCLUDE event count FROM SELECT COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case id as event count FROM event log WHERE event count less than or equal to 6

Name: Validation - PE
Text: DuckDB supports EXCLUDE which makes this very clean Alternatively the JOIN approach is universally understood and doesnt require EXCLUDE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets provide the JOIN approach as the primary solution since its highly standard and returns easily and also mention the Window Function approach with EXCLUDE as a modern DuckDB alternative
